rule: P4g raw-duplicate key and precision-duplicate fixture evidence
description: P4g says raw duplicates are identical concept+context+value, but does not state whether decimals is part of the key. Opus found no real same-context same-value differing-decimals precision pair in the 60-filing EXP-1 corpus, while the synthetic fixture proves the intended keep-highest and conflict branches.
why_two_ways: A production implementer could key raw duplicates without decimals and silently keep first-by-id, or key with decimals so same-value different-decimals rows fall through to the precision rule. The observed 60-filing corpus has zero behavior delta because no such real pair occurred.
fable_ruling: Accept synthetic-only precision-duplicate proof for EXP-1/O12 evidence. Do not widen the graph search. Proposed O12 pin: raw-duplicate key = identical concept, context, value, decimals; same-context same-value different-decimals pairs fall through to the precision rule and keep highest precision.
zero_behavior_delta: No EXP-1 rerun or gate reopen: no real differing-decimals pair appeared in the 60-filing corpus, and fixtures_exercised.json proves the intended code paths synthetically.
production_notes: Publish p4g_precision_kept at rollout so the real base rate is monitored continuously.
Add a production build-gate synthetic variant for the same-context different-decimals raw-duplicate boundary.
Record the D6 scope asymmetry: multi-registrant and null-pOR absence were graph-wide; precision-pair absence was only checked inside the 60-filing corpus.
